variant: give me the key stats on tretinoin results — numbers, not paragraphs

request.message: give me the key stats on tretinoin results — numbers, not paragraphs

response_body.response: don't see specific outcome numbers in your current docs — they focus on protocol (0.05% tretinoin, pea-sized, dry skin application) and mechanism (stimulates fibroblasts, increases collagen turnover, improves texture and pigmentation) rather than timeframes or percentage improvements. the protocol itself is there: start 0.05% tretinoin, apply on dry skin with a pea-sized amount, gradually increase frequency. for pigmentation specifically, pair it with dermastamping at 0.25mm, 2× per week on non-retinoid nights. if you want the clinical stats on tretinoin (typical timelines, % improvement in wrinkles/texture, etc.), that's. ask if you want me to pull that separately.

products.name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
products.description: OTC retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) — first-line for comedonal acne.
